Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your team can access inventory data anytime, anywhere, and keeps customer and supplier information secure and compliant with Singapore's PDPA rules. Your warehouse staff can check stock on their phones, your finance team can export data to accounting software, and you maintain a complete audit trail of who changed what. For a Singapore SME, this is non-negotiable: you need mobile access for fast-moving operations, PDPA compliance to avoid regulatory fines, and seamless data flow to your accountant.

Inventory Core.narrative: This family gives you a single source of truth for every item you stock — from SKU codes and quantities on hand to financial valuation and traceability through lot numbers. When you do a stock count, scan barcodes to catch discrepancies fast; when you need to report inventory value to your bank or auditor, the system calculates it automatically. For a Singapore SME managing multiple product lines or locations, this prevents costly stockouts, shrinkage, and accounting errors.

Procurement.narrative: This family streamlines how you order from suppliers — you create a purchase order, track its status, and manage supplier relationships all in one place. You can see which vendors deliver on time, negotiate better payment terms, and avoid duplicate orders or missed deadlines. For a Singapore SME relying on imports or multiple local suppliers, this cuts procurement cycle time and reduces the risk of supply disruptions.

Warehouse & Logistics.narrative: This family orchestrates the physical movement of goods — from receiving inbound shipments and storing them in the right location, to picking and packing customer orders and handling returns. You can operate multiple warehouses, dropship directly from suppliers, or assemble kits on demand, all with full visibility and traceability. For a Singapore SME with growing sales or complex fulfillment needs, this reduces picking errors, speeds up delivery, and improves customer satisfaction.

Planning & Compliance.narrative: This family helps you stay ahead of demand and stay compliant with regulations — automatic reorder points prevent stockouts, demand forecasting reduces overstock, and quality inspections catch defects before they reach customers. If you import goods, landed cost calculation ensures you know the true cost of each item, and trade compliance tools keep your customs paperwork in order. For a Singapore SME importing or exporting, this protects margins, avoids regulatory penalties, and builds customer trust.

Analytics & Intelligence.narrative: This family gives you dashboards and reports that show how fast inventory is turning, how often you fulfil orders on time, and where bottlenecks are in your supply chain. You can spot trends, compare supplier performance, and make data-driven decisions about stock levels and procurement. For a Singapore SME competing on speed and cost, this visibility helps you optimize working capital and respond quickly to market changes.

AI & Machine Learning for Supply Chain.narrative: This family uses advanced algorithms to predict demand from external signals (e.g., weather, social media trends) and detect anomalies in your supply chain automatically. It is a nice-to-have for mature, data-rich operations but requires significant data history and technical expertise to set up and maintain. For most Singapore SMEs, this is a future investment — focus first on getting the core inventory and planning capabilities right.
